high.title: artist('')/bandcamp('') accept empty/whitespace key and silently hit the wrong route — asymmetric with resolve('')
high.fix: classifyArtistKey('')/('   ') trims to '' which matches none of HEX64/LOCATOR/DIGITS, so it returns {type:'direct', key:''}. client.ts then builds /api/v1/artist/ and bandcamp('') builds /api/v1/bandcamp/ (encodeURIComponent('')===''), colliding with the sibling bulk collection route /api/v1/bandcamp rather than failing fast. resolveQueryToParam (identity.ts:44) explicitly treats '' as absent and throws exactly_one_of, so the SDK guards empty input on resolve() but NOT on artist()/bandcamp() — and ADX-1 (sdd.md:241) mandates the resolve('') guard. Add a fail-fast guard in classifyArtistKey: if (s === '') throw new CrateValidationError('crate.artist(): key must not be empty', { code:'exactly_one_of', param:'key', hint:'pass a cluster_id, slug, name, or discogs:/mbid: locator', next:"crate.artist('Four Tet')" }); and apply the same empty check at the bandcamp(artistKey) entry (client.ts:205).

high.title: CratePaginationError.masterId-equivalent: contract Error field master_id is extracted but dropped by toJSON / absent from CrateErrorJSON (ADX-2 handoff loses a real contract field)
high.fix: The live contract's Error schema declares master_id (openapi.json components.schemas.Error.properties.master_id: number — verified), so error-mapping.ts:78 correctly extracts it onto CrateAPIError.masterId (errors.ts:209). But CrateAPIError.toJSON (errors.ts:242-250) never serializes it, CrateErrorJSON (errors.ts:119-136) has no masterId field, and CRATE_ERROR_REGISTRY.api.carries (errors.ts:71) omits it. An agent branching on the live error sees masterId; one round-tripping through the documented JSON-safe handoff path (ADX-2) silently loses it. Add masterId?: number to CrateErrorJSON and `if (this.masterId !== undefined) json.masterId = this.masterId;` to CrateAPIError.toJSON, and add 'masterId' to the api registry carries list.

high.title: pagination_malformed_page .next is prose, not a copy-pasteable call — violates ADX-4 (a must for client-side throws)
high.fix: SDD §15 ADX-4 (sdd.md:244) mandates that EVERY client-side throw carry a .next that is 'a copy-pasteable corrected call'. CratePaginationError is clientSide:true (errors.ts:104-109). The malformed-page throw sets next:'retry the call or report the source to crate support' — pure prose, no method name, no argument form (pagination.ts:69). Make it a real resume call, e.g. next: `crate.bandcamp.bulk({ cursor: ${JSON.stringify(state.cursor)} })` and move the 'report to support' text into hint. For pagination_no_progress (pagination.ts:85), drop the trailing ' to inspect, then report to crate support' so .next is the bare runnable call; keep that advice in hint.

medium.title: Pagination cycle-detection misses a cycle back to the initial resume cursor (SDD §6 'previously-seen cursor' not fully honored)
medium.fix: In makeBulkIterable.pageGen (src/pagination.ts), `seen` is only populated with each fetched page's next_cursor (line 88); the initial resume cursor params.cursor (used as nextCursor for page 1 at line 45) is never added. The immediate-cycle guard (line 80) compares advanced===nextCursor only. If the server walks several pages and then returns next_cursor equal to the ORIGINAL resume cursor, neither guard fires → infinite loop — exactly the failure SDD §6 (sdd.md:165, 'or a previously-seen cursor') exists to prevent. Tests cover only the immediate self-loop. Fix: after `let nextCursor = params.cursor ?? undefined;` (line 45) add `if (nextCursor !== undefined) seen.add(nextCursor);` so seen.has(advanced) (line 80) also catches a return to the initial cursor.

rationale: Four findings survive verification as material; none rises to a BLOCKER (no data corruption, auth bypass, injection, or RCE — the SDK is a thin read client over HTTPS to a single trusted host). I confirmed each against source + the live contract. The three HIGH items are all real surface/conformance defects that tests miss: (1) artist('')/bandcamp('') silently hit a sibling collection route while the parallel resolve('') path is correctly guarded per ADX-1 — a genuine fail-fast asymmetry verified at identity.ts:70-78 vs identity.ts:44; (2) master_id is a CONTRACT-DECLARED Error field (verified in openapi.json components.schemas.Error) that the SDK extracts but the ADX-2 toJSON handoff drops, so live-object vs serialized-object disagree on a real field; (3) the malformed-page .next is prose, directly violating the ADX-4 'must' in sdd.md:244. The MEDIUM (initial-cursor cycle) is a clear SDD §6 'previously-seen cursor' gap (sdd.md:165) with a one-line fix. I downgraded four reported items to non-actionable: the 1ms-exhausted-attempt (http.ts:226) is gated in normal flow by the delay>=remaining() guards (lines 243/281) and only bites pathological totalDeadlineMs=0 callers; the .raw UTF-16 cap (http.ts:87) is bounded and not a leak; the X-API-Key-on-beacons (http.ts:73-74) goes to the same trusted host and SDD's 'distinct from X-API-Key' (sdd.md:125) describes the token's nature, not wire exclusivity; the server-cursor echo into .next (pagination.ts:85) is JSON.stringify-escaped and self-assessed acceptable. The concurrent bulkAll-state finding (pagination.ts:40) is a real but documented-single-consumer concern worth a doc note, not a fix. The ADX-9 missing @example for bare-numeric artist() is a doc nicety, not material. Recommend landing the three HIGH fixes (each one-to-few lines) plus the cycle-seeding MEDIUM before publish; all are surgical and test-backable.
security_summary: Posture is sound for an SDK threat model. Zero runtime deps (no supply-chain surface beyond Node). Secrets handling is correct: apiKey/beaconToken go only into headers (http.ts:73-74), never into URLs or logs; .raw is size-capped (RAW_CAP=2048, http.ts:10,87,105) and toJSON deliberately omits .raw and reduces .cause to {name,message} (errors.ts:182-197), so response bodies/headers don't leak into agent-to-agent handoff envelopes. No injection vectors: query values go through URL.searchParams (http.ts:55-59), path segments through encodeURIComponent (client.ts:207,259,266 etc.), and the one server-origin string surfaced to agents (the cursor in CratePaginationError.next, pagination.ts:85) is JSON.stringify-escaped and never enters a URL or header — residual risk is only theoretical prompt-injection content reaching an agent reading .next, acceptably low. No ReDoS: HEX64/LOCATOR/DIGITS/URL_RE (identity.ts:6-10) are linear, anchored, no nested quantifiers. No prototype-pollution sink (body fields read by explicit key, never spread into a prototype-reachable target). No SSRF beyond the configured baseUrl (a base-url-has-path guard exists per the error registry). One minor wire-hygiene note (X-API-Key sent alongside the beacon Bearer, http.ts:73-74) is to the same trusted host over HTTPS — not a leak. No BLOCKER- or HIGH-severity security issues.
